title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.h1: Authentic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Adas (Lebanese Red Lentil Soup)?
Why You'll Love This Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Adas Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of warm Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas), a healthy and flavorful Middle Eastern classic.

description: This hearty Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a beloved Middle Eastern comfort food. It's a vibrant and nutritious dish, bursting with flavors of lemon, garlic, and fresh herbs. Perfect for a chilly evening or a light, wholesome meal, Adas offers a taste of authentic Lebanese home cooking that is both satisfying and incredibly healthy.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and celery. Sauté for 5-7 minutes, until the vegetables soften and the onion becomes translucent.

instructions.title: Add Garlic and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, and turmeric powder (if using). Cook for another 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the spices are fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic.

instructions.title: Introduce Lentils and Broth
instructions.text: Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the sautéed vegetables and spices. Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let the soup simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Season and Blend (Optional)
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the salt and black pepper. For a smoother soup, use an immersion blender to blend a portion or all of the soup directly in the pot, or carefully transfer to a regular blender in batches. Adjust consistency with a little more hot water or broth if needed.

instructions.title: Finish with Lemon and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasonings as necessary. Ladle the hot soup into bowls and garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro or mint. Serve immediately with a side of pita bread or a fresh salad.

tips: Always rinse your red lentils thoroughly before cooking to remove any debris and improve digestibility.
The key to authentic Adas is a generous amount of fresh lemon juice added at the end; it brightens the entire soup.
For an extra layer of flavor, consider a garlic-mint 'ta'leeya' by frying minced garlic and dried mint in olive oil, then drizzling it over individual servings just before eating.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of the Middle East with a bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas. This humble yet incredibly flavorful soup is a cornerstone of Lebanese cuisine, cherished for its simplicity, nourishing qualities, and comforting warmth. Known simply as "Adas" in Arabic, this vibrant red lentil soup is more than just a meal; it's a taste of home, a staple enjoyed by families across Lebanon and beyond. Whether you're seeking a hearty plant-based meal, a quick weeknight dinner, or simply a bowl of pure comfort, this traditional recipe is sure to become a cherished addition to your kitchen repertoire.

## What is Adas (Lebanese Red Lentil Soup)?

Adas, which translates directly to "lentils" in Arabic, refers to this iconic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup. It's a velvety, often slightly chunky, and incredibly aromatic soup made primarily from red lentils. Unlike some other lentil soups that might be heavy with meat or a multitude of vegetables, Lebanese Adas focuses on letting the earthy flavor of the lentils shine, brightened by a generous squeeze of lemon juice and the warm embrace of cumin. It’s characteristically a vibrant orange-red hue, thanks to the lentils, and boasts a wonderfully smooth texture, though some prefer it with a bit more bite. This soup is a testament to the power of simple, wholesome ingredients coming together to create something truly extraordinary and deeply satisfying.

## Why You'll Love This Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

There are countless reasons to fall in love with this traditional Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas. Firstly, it's incredibly healthy, packed with plant-based protein, dietary fiber, and essential nutrients, making it a fantastic choice for a wholesome meal. It's naturally vegan and gluten-free, catering to a wide range of dietary needs without compromising on flavor. Beyond its health benefits, this soup is remarkably easy and quick to prepare, often ready in under 30 minutes, making it perfect for busy weeknights. Its affordability is another major draw; basic pantry staples are all you need. But perhaps the most compelling reason is its unparalleled comfort factor. The warm, tangy, and subtly spiced broth, combined with tender lentils, creates a deeply satisfying experience that feels like a hug in a bowl, especially on a chilly day.

## Ingredients for Lebanese Red Lentil Soup

The beauty of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup lies in its minimal, yet impactful, ingredient list. You'll need:

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Ensure they are split red lentils, as they cook quickly and break down easily.
*   **Olive Oil:** A good quality extra virgin olive oil for sautéing and drizzling.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation that provides depth of flavor.
*   **Vegetable Broth or Water:** Vegetable broth adds more flavor, but water works perfectly well, especially with good seasoning.
*   **Cumin:** The essential spice that gives Adas its signature warm, earthy notes.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice:** Crucial for brightening the soup and balancing the earthiness of the lentils. Do not skip this!
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste, enhancing all the other flavors.
*   **Optional Garnishes:** Fresh cilantro or parsley, extra lemon wedges, a drizzle of olive oil, or crispy pita bread for serving.

These simple ingredients, when combined correctly, create a symphony of flavors that define this beloved Middle Eastern soup.

## How to Make Lebanese Red Lentil Soup: Step-by-Step

Making a delicious bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is straightforward. Follow these steps for perfect results:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any impurities and excess starch. Drain well.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat a generous drizzle of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Combine Ingredients:** Stir in the rinsed lentils, cumin, salt, and black pepper. Pour in the vegetable broth (or water). Bring the mixture to a boil.
4.  **Simmer:** Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
5.  **Blend (Optional):** For a smoother consistency, you can use an immersion blender directly in the pot to blend the soup to your desired texture. Alternatively, carefully transfer portions of the soup to a regular blender (blending in batches, never overfilling, and holding the lid down with a towel due to hot liquids) and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Some prefer a slightly chunky soup, so blending is entirely up to your preference.
6.  **Finish and Serve:** Remove the soup from the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed – you might want more salt, pepper, or lemon. Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas into bowls, garnish with fresh herbs or a drizzle of olive oil, and serve immediately.

## Tips for the Best Adas Soup

To elevate your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup to its absolute best, consider these tips:

*   **Don't Skimp on Lemon:** Fresh lemon juice is non-negotiable. It provides the essential brightness and tang that cuts through the richness of the lentils and truly defines Adas. Add it at the very end to preserve its fresh flavor.
*   **Rinse Those Lentils:** Thoroughly rinsing lentils removes starch and impurities, leading to a cleaner flavor and better texture.
*   **Quality Olive Oil:** A good quality extra virgin olive oil enhances the overall flavor profile, especially when used for sautéing and as a finishing drizzle.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** If the soup is too thick after cooking, simply add a splash more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. If it's too thin, simmer it uncovered for a few more minutes to reduce slightly.
*   **Bloom Your Cumin:** For an extra layer of flavor, briefly toast your cumin seeds in a dry pan before grinding them, or gently heat ground cumin in the olive oil with the onions for a minute before adding other ingredients. This "blooming" releases its full aroma.
*   **Let Flavors Marry:** Like many soups, Adas often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld and deepen.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. It makes a satisfying light meal on its own, especially when paired with some warm pita bread for dipping. You can also serve it as a comforting appetizer before a larger Middle Eastern feast.

*   **Garnish:** A fresh sprinkle of chopped cilantro or parsley adds a burst of color and freshness. A final drizzle of good quality olive oil enhances richness. Some like to add a pinch of dried mint or a swirl of plain yogurt (if not strictly vegan).
*   **Bread:** Warm pita bread, either plain or lightly toasted, is the perfect accompaniment for scooping up every last drop. You can also make pita croutons by toasting cubed pita with olive oil and a sprinkle of sumac.
*   **Salad:** A simple side salad, such as a crisp cucumber and tomato salad dressed with lemon and olive oil, provides a refreshing contrast to the hearty soup.
*   **Olives:** A small bowl of Kalamata or other briny olives can complement the soup beautifully.

## Storage and Reheating

One of the great things about Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is how well it stores, making it ideal for meal prepping or enjoying leftovers.

*   **Refrigeration:** Allow the soup to cool completely before transferring it to an airtight container. It will keep well in the refrigerator for 3-4 days.
*   **Freezing:** Adas freezes exceptionally well. Portion the cooled soup into freezer-safe containers or bags, leaving a little headspace for expansion. It can be frozen for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator before reheating.
*   **Reheating:** Reheat gently on the stovetop over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally. If the soup has thickened considerably (which it often does), add a splash of water or vegetable broth to achieve your desired consistency. You can also reheat individual portions in the microwave.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas is inherently adaptable, allowing for personal touches and dietary needs.

*   **Spice it Up:** For a bit of heat, add a pinch of red pepper flakes or a tiny amount of cayenne pepper when sautéing the aromatics. A touch of turmeric can also add a beautiful color and subtle earthy note.
*   **Add Vegetables:** While traditional Adas is quite simple, you can certainly add finely diced carrots or celery along with the onions for extra nutrients and texture. Some even add a small diced potato for a creamier consistency.
*   **Different Herbs:** While cilantro and parsley are common garnishes, you can experiment with fresh mint stirred in at the end for a different flavor profile.
*   **Vegan/Vegetarian:** This recipe is naturally vegan and vegetarian, making it a fantastic plant-based option without any modifications needed, provided you use vegetable broth.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Red lentils are naturally gluten-free, and as long as you use a certified gluten-free vegetable broth, your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas will be entirely gluten-free.
*   **Texture Control:** If you prefer a very smooth, creamy soup, blend it thoroughly. For a soup with more body and texture, blend only half of it or mash some of the lentils against the side of the pot with a spoon.

Embrace the simplicity and rich flavors of this traditional Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas. It’s a dish that nourishes the body and soul, bringing a taste of authentic Middle Eastern comfort to your table with every spoonful.
